title: carex aquatilis var. altior
intro: Carex aquatilis var. altior, also known as the Alaskan sedge or tall water sedge, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Carex genus. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Carex aquatilis var. altior include its ability to help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and pneumonia, as well as its potential to aid in wound healing. In herbal preparations, Carex aquatilis var. altior is often made into a decoction or infusion, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
